class TradeGovApi
  def self.lookup(name)
    encoded_name = URI.encode_uri_component(name)
    url = "https://data.trade.gov/consolidated_screening_list/v1/search?name=#{encoded_name}"
    response = Faraday.get(url) do |req|
      req.headers["subscription-key"] = Anedot.secrets.trade_gov[:subscription_key]
    end.body
    JSON.parse(response)
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

# Google Chat HTTP client
module GoogleChat
  # Public: Client for communicating with Google Chat webhooks
  class Client
    attr_reader :chat_url, :text

    def self.post(chat_url, text)
      new(chat_url, text).post
    end

    def self.deliver(message)
      post(message.chat_url, message.text)
    end

    def initialize(chat_url, text)
      @chat_url = chat_url
      @text = text
    end

    def post
      return if chat_url.blank?

      chat_uri = ::Addressable::URI.parse(chat_url)
      conn(chat_uri).post do |req|
        req.url chat_uri.request_uri
        req.body = { text: text }
      end
    end

  private

    def conn(uri)
      request_host = "#{uri.scheme}://#{uri.host}"
      Faraday.new(url: request_host, ssl: { verify: true }) do |conn|
        conn.request :json
        conn.request :instrumentation
        conn.use Faraday::Response::Logger, Rails.logger
        conn.use Faraday::Response::RaiseError
        conn.adapter :typhoeus
      end
    end
  end
end

    # Public method to provision API access for a user

# frozen_string_literal: true

module Wrappers
  module ActiveCampaign
    # request obj
    class Request
      def initialize(client, method, path, opts)
        @configuration = client.configuration
        @method = method
        @path = path
        @body = format_request_body(opts[:body] || {})
        @timeout_minutes = opts[:timeout_minutes] || 1
      end

      def perform
        connection.public_send(@method, @path, @method != :get && @body)
      end

    private

      def format_request_body(body)
        body.deep_transform_keys! { |k| k.to_s.camelcase(:lower) }
        body.to_json
      end

      def connection
        Faraday.new(url: @configuration.url, ssl: { verify: true }) do |conn|
          conn.request :instrumentation, name: "request.active_campaign"
          conn.use Faraday::Response::Logger, Rails.logger
          conn.options[:timeout] = @timeout_minutes * 60
          conn.options[:open_timeout] = @timeout_minutes * 60
          conn.headers[:api_token] = @configuration.api_token
          conn.headers[:content_type] = "application/json"
          conn.headers[:user_agent] = "Anedot/#{Anedot::REVISION[0, 8]}"
          conn.headers[:x_integration_id] = @configuration.integration_uid
          conn.adapter :typhoeus
        end
      end
    end
  end
end

**Clarifying request body signing and JSON handling**

To ensure consistent signing, I'll convert the payload to a JSON string manually and assign it directly as the request body, avoiding double encoding by Faraday's `request :json` middleware. This keeps the signed bytes stable while preserving instrumentation since Faraday skips encoding if the body is already a string. I'll design a simple class with a general `invoke_model` method accepting a JSON body and optional model ID for flexible RESTful requests to Amazon Bedrock.

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: lib/bedrock_api.rb
@@
 # frozen_string_literal: true
 
 # AWS Bedrock API client
 class BedrockApi
-  def initialize
-    todo
+  SERVICE = "bedrock"
+  DEFAULT_ACCEPT = "application/json"
+  DEFAULT_CONTENT_TYPE = "application/json"
+
+  def initialize(access_key_id: Anedot.secrets.bedrock[:access_key_id],
+                 secret_access_key: Anedot.secrets.bedrock[:secret_access_key],
+                 region: Anedot.secrets.bedrock[:region])
+    @access_key_id = access_key_id
+    @secret_access_key = secret_access_key
+    @region = region
+  end
+
+  def invoke_model(body:, model_id: Anedot.secrets.bedrock[:model_id], accept: DEFAULT_ACCEPT,
+                   content_type: DEFAULT_CONTENT_TYPE)
+    payload = body.is_a?(String) ? body : body.to_json
+    path = "/model/#{model_id}/invoke"
+
+    connection.post(path) do |req|
+      req.headers.update(signed_headers(path:, body: payload, content_type:))
+      req.headers["accept"] = accept
+      req.body = payload
+    end
   end
+
+private
+
+  def connection
+    @connection ||= Faraday.new(url: endpoint, ssl: { verify: true }) do |conn|
+      conn.request :instrumentation, name: "request.bedrock"
+      conn.use Faraday::Response::Logger, Rails.logger
+      conn.use Faraday::Response::RaiseError
+      conn.adapter :typhoeus
+    end
+  end
+
+  def signed_headers(path:, body:, content_type:)
+    signer.sign_request(
+      http_method: "POST",
+      url: "#{endpoint}#{path}",
+      headers: { "content-type" => content_type },
+      body:
+    ).headers
+  end
+
+  def signer
+    @signer ||= Aws::Sigv4::Signer.new(
+      service: SERVICE,
+      region: @region,
+      access_key_id: @access_key_id,
+      secret_access_key: @secret_access_key
+    )
+  end
+
+  def endpoint
+    "https://bedrock-runtime.#{@region}.amazonaws.com"
+  end
 end
*** End Patch
